user_prompt: WifiManager needs public calls for initiating a connection to a specific hotspot as specified by a wifi_item_t, initiating softAP as specified by a wifi_item_t, scanning for SSIDs and automatically connecting to an existing wifi_item_t if found, disconnecting, and getting status.
agent_reply: Implemented the WiFi control calls in [WifiManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.h) and [WifiManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp).
- `connectToHotspot()` starts station mode connection to a specific configured hotspot.
- `startSoftAp()` starts AP mode from a configured AP item.
- `scanAndConnect()` scans visible SSIDs, matches them against configured station entries, and starts connecting to the first configured match.
- `status()` reports idle, connecting, connected, AP, scan failure, connect failure, etc.

user_prompt: WifiManager needs onConnect and onDisconnect callbacks that can be registered from a parent, and the wifi_item_t that is relevant needs to be passed into the callbacks
agent_reply: Implemented the Wi-Fi connect/disconnect callback path.
- Added `poll()` to detect station/AP connect and disconnect transitions.
One note: the callback receives a pointer owned by `WifiManager`, so it is valid as long as the loaded Wi-Fi list remains alive and unchanged.
Implemented the Wi-Fi connect/disconnect callback path.
- Added `poll()` to detect station/AP connect and disconnect transitions.
One note: the callback receives a pointer owned by `WifiManager`, so it is valid as long as the loaded Wi-Fi list remains alive and unchanged.

user_prompt: This needs to spin up a FreeRTOS task thread on the same executing core as the current executing core
The thread will remember the time retrieved from the server, and then determine how far off the current time is from RTC is from NTP time.
There is a public call for initiating this, another call for checking status (idle, busy, done, error), another call for cancelling, and another call for retreving the error.
There needs to be a public call for reading how far off the RTC is from NTP time, and another call that can initiate a write to the RTC.
There needs to be a way for a parent to register a `onComplete` callback, which is fired and passes in the results.

user_prompt: ok, I want to make a general usage flow change. the device will always be connectable but not discoverable, pairing mode will make it discoverable. Activating Wi-Fi will shut everything down. There will no longer need to be a waiting mode in addition to pairing mode.
I don't see this mentioned in my design brainstorm documents, if I did and I missed it, warn me
agent_reply: I did find the old idea in two places:
I updated those. The only remaining “wait for incoming connection” text is in [ui-design.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md), but that is about Wi-Fi AP mode, so I left it alone.
- Bluetooth now initializes as connectable but non-discoverable.
- Pairing mode makes it discoverable temporarily.
- Successful pairing closes discovery and returns to connectable/non-discoverable.
- Wi-Fi station/AP/scan activation now stops audio, stops recording, and shuts down Bluetooth first.

commit_message: change bluetooth init behavior to be connectable

user_prompt: Make it only start the scan as async, have `void WifiManager::poll()` handle that particular state, and check the results when the scan is finished, poll is then responsible for calling for a connection if needed
agent_reply: Done. `WifiManager::scanAndConnect()` now starts an async scan and returns immediately once the scan is running.
- searches scan results for a configured SSID
- calls `connectToHotspot(..., false)` when it finds one
- `status()` preserves `StationScanning` so UI/status callers can see that transitional state.

user_prompt: have a `setOnScanFinished` function for registering a callback for when scan finishes, have it supply the `wifi_item_t` representing what was found, instead of automatically connecting, let the callback handle the connecting if the parent wants to
agent_reply: - When the scan completes, `poll()` searches for a configured station.
- It calls `setOnScanFinished`’s callback with the matching `wifi_item_t*`.
- If no configured station was found, it calls the callback with `nullptr`.
- It no longer automatically calls `connectToHotspot()` after scan completion.

- Wi-Fi modal shows SSID and local IP.

commit_message: starting to add a web server

user_prompt: complete `uint32_t generate_8_digit_nounce()` and the AP random password generation will use it, with leading 0 padding, so that it's always 8 characters
agent_reply: [utilfuncs.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.cpp:405) now completes `generate_8_digit_nounce()` using `esp_random()`, keeps the stub’s intended low-byte hash derived from bits `31:8`, and retries if the final value would exceed eight decimal digits. I exposed it in [utilfuncs.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.h:43).
[WifiManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:319) now uses it for generated AP passwords with zero-padded decimal formatting:
I also changed [WifiManager.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.h:32) so the generated AP password length is `8`, making the stored buffer exactly eight characters plus terminator.

One security note: the low-byte hash makes part of that eight-digit value dependent on the other bytes, so this has less independent randomness than a fully random eight-digit password. That matches the stub you left, but it matters if this AP password is meant to carry much security weight.

[utilfuncs.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.cpp:405) now completes `generate_8_digit_nounce()` using `esp_random()`, keeps the stub’s intended low-byte hash derived from bits `31:8`, and retries if the final value would exceed eight decimal digits. I exposed it in [utilfuncs.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.h:43).

commit_message: prepping for adding NVS config store

user_prompt: I am asking for a big change to both `WifiManager` and `BtHostList`
Previously, the WiFi credentials lived in a JSON file on the microSD card. This is of course vulnerable.
I want all configurations to live inside NVS when the build has security enabled
I have started to sprinkle the flag "BUILD_WITH_SECURITY" in a lot of places.

So instead of a linked list that is loaded from JSON, we will use structures with a fixed number of entries. The strings are fixed length instead of allocated on demand. 8 entries for everything should be more than enough.
* make sure the string lengths make sense for the data it needs to hold
* add constants for the string lengths so the code doesn't have hard coded numbers
* implement the loading and saving of the structures through NVS
* make everything is still compatible with current code

The bluetooth part of this has some added features, like a user customized name vs reported name storage, and a last used time. For last used time, only if the storage is full and pairing happens, the oldest unbonded entry is overwritten. If all entries are bonded, then the oldest bonded entry is overwritten.

there shall be two implementations of `loadFromMicroSd`, with `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, it loads the JSON but it loads it into NVS if existing, if not existing, no error is shown. Without `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, it loads as it does now.
agent_reply: Implemented the secure NVS-backed paths for both managers.
- [WifiManager.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.h:7) now defines fixed string-length constants and 8-slot secure storage for stations, APs, and cloud endpoints.

- [WifiManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:765) now loads NVS first under `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, optionally imports `/wifi.json` into NVS if present, and treats missing JSON as non-fatal.

- [BtHostList.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.h:8) now defines fixed Bluetooth host storage, including display name, custom name, reported name, bond state, last-used time, and 8 slots.
- [BtHostList.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.cpp:444) now imports `/bluetooth.json` into NVS under `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, with missing JSON non-fatal.

- [BtHostList.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.cpp:797) now supports secure fixed-slot insert/remove. If full, pairing overwrites oldest unbonded first, otherwise oldest bonded.
- Kept compatibility fields like `ssid`, `password`, `name`, and `next_node` so current UI/test/main code still works.
- Normal `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest` compiles touched C++ and still fails only at the existing IRAM overflow: `.iram0.text` overflowed by `2052 bytes`.

- Direct secure-branch compile with `-DBUILD_WITH_SECURITY` passed for `WifiManager.cpp`, `BtHostList.cpp`, `main.cpp`, `CloudUpload.cpp`, `ScrollView.cpp`, `BluetoothManager.cpp`, and `test_bootreadfiles.cpp`.
Implemented the secure NVS-backed paths for both managers.

- [WifiManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:765) now loads NVS first under `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, optionally imports `/wifi.json` into NVS if present, and treats missing JSON as non-fatal.

- [BtHostList.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.h:8) now defines fixed Bluetooth host storage, including display name, custom name, reported name, bond state, last-used time, and 8 slots.
- [BtHostList.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.cpp:444) now imports `/bluetooth.json` into NVS under `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, with missing JSON non-fatal.

- [BtHostList.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.cpp:797) now supports secure fixed-slot insert/remove. If full, pairing overwrites oldest unbonded first, otherwise oldest bonded.
- Kept compatibility fields like `ssid`, `password`, `name`, and `next_node` so current UI/test/main code still works.
- Normal `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest` compiles touched C++ and still fails only at the existing IRAM overflow: `.iram0.text` overflowed by `2052 bytes`.

- Direct secure-branch compile with `-DBUILD_WITH_SECURITY` passed for `WifiManager.cpp`, `BtHostList.cpp`, `main.cpp`, `CloudUpload.cpp`, `ScrollView.cpp`, `BluetoothManager.cpp`, and `test_bootreadfiles.cpp`.

user_prompt: I want to run `void test_sdcard()` to test performance of the recorder with encryption enabled
make sure everything is wired so that it works with encryption actually built and active
agent_reply: Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.
- installs a deterministic test master key only if no key already exists,
- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.

commit_message: some work on secured web interactions

user_prompt: implement the C++ handler for "/set_cfg", it needs the same timestamp authentication helper, and it handles a large binary blob that is encrypted before decryption, reject it if it is over 64kb, decrypt it into something that ArduinoJSON can handle, then have it parse it into objects. Assume the frontend sends back an object with two main keys "network" and "bluetooth" similar to the JSON we've sent out. Use `BtHostList` and `WifiManager` functions to save them into NVS, implement functions as needed

Remember, we have agreed to operate on a monolithic blob of data, not streaming, even though the "/get_cfg" handler is streaming, you have recommended to change that one later to sending a monolithic blob as well.
A rule: a blank or missing wifi password means "do not change existing". As a policy, open wifi networks may not be used, and passwords are a bit "write only"
agent_reply: Implemented `/set_cfg` as a monolithic encrypted binary config upload.
- Decrypts into a PSRAM-preferred buffer, null-terminates it, then parses with ArduinoJson.
- Applies `"network"` and `"bluetooth"` objects into NVS via new manager commit helpers.
- blank/missing password preserves existing password by SSID
- new Wi-Fi entry without a password is rejected
- open/invalid-password Wi-Fi entries are rejected on network config updates
- Added disconnect cleanup so an aborted upload will not leave the config upload state stuck.
Build passed. I also restored the generated `src/version.c` change after the build. Unrelated dirty files like `.vscode/extensions.json` and the btramusage test files were left untouched.

- new Wi-Fi entry without a password is rejected
- open/invalid-password Wi-Fi entries are rejected on network config updates
- Added disconnect cleanup so an aborted upload will not leave the config upload state stuck.
Build passed. I also restored the generated `src/version.c` change after the build. Unrelated dirty files like `.vscode/extensions.json` and the btramusage test files were left untouched.

user_prompt: cleanly remove the requirement of password for cloud uploads, the security design has changed
agent_reply: Implemented. Cloud upload destinations no longer require or store a password/salt.
- Legacy non-secure upload hash is now filename + timestamp only.
- Secure upload path still uses `Aegis::hmacSha256` with the master key.
- Added secure NVS migration for old network config blobs that still had the cloud password field, dropping that field and saving the new layout.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` churn afterward.

also, get rid of any code that deals with `legacy` nvs storage, we do not need to care about that until development has reached some form of release

- Removed old `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY` branches and old linked-list compatibility paths from Wi-Fi/Bluetooth config code.
- Removed Wi-Fi and Bluetooth legacy NVS migration handling.
- Fixed fixed-entry structs fallout: removed `next_node` usage and cloud `name` usage.
- Level 0: `/get_cfg` and `/set_cfg` use plain JSON, cloud upload auth uses per-cloud password.
- Level 1+: config transport stays session encrypted, cloud upload auth uses `network-key`.
- FTP only starts when `BUILD_FTP_SERVER` is set and `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL <= 0`.
- Boot now loads Wi-Fi/Bluetooth JSON only for level 0; higher levels load NVS directly.
- Cloud list labels now use URL instead of removed cloud name.
- `git diff --check` on the files I edited passed.
- All four got through compilation and failed only at the known link-stage IRAM0 issue: `.iram0.text` overflowed by `2104 bytes`.

user_prompt: I know you just deleted it, but we need a `WifiApModeView` that is a `FlyGuiView`. This will display Wi-Fi credentials while in soft AP mode.
Place `sprit_wifi_50` in the top left corner (but not covering the top bar). If this is the default super safe randomly generated soft AP, then also put `sprite_security_50` beside it.

The bottom right corner, also linked to `BtnC`, put in a clickable `FlyGuiItem` that switches between `sprit_eye_closed_100` and `sprit_eye_open_100` when touched. Open eye means the info on the screen is displayed, closed means the sensitive parts are displayed as "**********"
Below the Wi-Fi icon, there should be 5 lines of text, similar to below
IP: <IP of the internal web server, probably 192.168.4.1>
Below the IP line, use the smallest font, show a line of text that cycles through a few displayed data items, cycling every 3 seconds, the items are:
* "page loads: n", number of times the "/get_info" request has been served since starting the AP
* "logins: n", number of times the "/get_cfg" request has been served since starting the AP, it is not displayed under security-level 0
* "saves: n", number of times the "/set_cfg" request (also the reset password and reset memory) has been served since starting the AP
* "errors: n", number of times there has been an apparent error, such as security-level mismatch error, or, some form of authentication error, those are the important ones
* "downloads: n", number of times a file has been downloaded, also track deletions and uploads with this number even though it doesn't make sense
`WifiManager` needs getters for these, and they need to be reset when the Wi-Fi starts, there may be request URL handlers missing but we'll implement those later

I am aware that I did not include some sort of stop button. The user must either click the physical reset button or power button to exit. So add a red line of text, small, as the next line, hinting so. I have no idea if it fits on the screen but do it so I can see.
agent_reply: - Shows `sprit_wifi_50` at the top-left below the top bar.
- Shows `sprit_security_50` beside it for the generated secure AP.
- Shows SSID/password/IP, with SSID/password hidden as `**********` by default.
- Adds the bottom-right eye toggle using `sprit_eye_closed_100` / `sprit_eye_open_100`, touchable and also tied to `BtnC`.
- Cycles the small stats line every 3 seconds.
- Adds the red “reset or power off” exit hint.
- Added “Default Secure AP” to the Wi-Fi scroll list.
- Selecting it starts `WifiManager::startGeneratedSoftAp()` and shows the AP view.
- Selecting a configured AP starts that AP and shows the same view.
- Added `WifiManager` web counter getters and reset-on-AP-start behavior.
- Counted `/get_info`, `/get_cfg`, `/set_cfg`, reset password/memory, downloads/deletes/uploads, and notable errors.

- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` and `pio run -e thefly_sec1` both compile the changed files successfully, then still fail at final link with the existing IRAM overflow: `iram0_0_seg` overflowed by `2152 bytes`.

user_prompt: in `lib\FlyGuiViews\WifiApModeView.cpp`, probably at x=104 and y just below the top bar, show two lines of text, in the smallest font
the text should be "<mac of connected wifi client or nobody>" then next line is just a number
the number is the number of times any client has connected
agent_reply: `WifiApModeView` now draws two smallest-font lines at the top-right area starting at `x=104`:
- connected SoftAP client MAC, formatted with hyphens, or `nobody`

`WifiManager` now tracks the current SoftAP client via `esp_wifi_ap_get_sta_list()` and increments the count whenever a client appears after no client, or a different client appears. The tracking resets when Wi-Fi/AP starts or stops.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` succeeded. The first build run had a transient missing `version.c.o` artifact, rerunning regenerated it and linked cleanly.

`WifiManager` now tracks the current SoftAP client via `esp_wifi_ap_get_sta_list()` and increments the count whenever a client appears after no client, or a different client appears. The tracking resets when Wi-Fi/AP starts or stops.
